content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 15, 1990

Honorable Peter R. Chacon
Chairman
Committee on Elections, Reapportionment
and Constitutional Amendments
State Capitol
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA  94249-0001

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-110

Dear Assemblymember Chacon:

This is in response to your request for affirmation of the
Assembly's application of Section 81012 of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act")1 to bills being heard on the Assembly floor.

Section 81012(a) provides:

(a) This title may be amended to further its purposes by
statute, passed in each house by roll call vote entered
in the journal, two-thirds of the membership concurring
and signed by the Governor, if at least 12 days prior to
passage in each house the bill in its final form has
been delivered to the commission for distribution to the
news media and to every person who has requested the
commission to send copies of such bills to him or her.

(Emphasis added.)


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-90-110
Page 2

The Assembly calculation of the 12-day notice required in the
Act is in conformance with Section 6800, which provides:

The time in which any act provided by law is to be done
is computed by excluding the first day, and including
the last, unless the last day is a holiday, and then it
is also excluded.

Thus, to comply with the 12-day notice requirement in the Act, the
twelve days begin on the day after the bill, in printed form, is
delivered to the Commission. The bill may then be heard on the
floor of the House on the twelfth day after delivery. To cite
your example, if the Clerk of the Assembly were to deliver a bill
in its final form to the Commission on February 1, the Assembly
could vote on the passage of that bill on February 13.

We would also like to clarify that a bill, in its final print
form, may be delivered to the Commission's offices at any time
during working hours, Monday through Friday, between 8:00 a.m. and
5:00 p.m., except on legal holidays.

Thank you for seeking clarification of this important rule.
If we can be of further assistance, please contact me at 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Lilly Spitz
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:LS:ld
